text.title: DDD ACL Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to add a new external integration behind an ACL.

step.name: identify-semantic-gap
step.action: Compare external model with domain terms. If field names, semantics, or error shapes materially differ, build an ACL. If identical, route to direct-call methodology and STOP.

step.name: design-domain-interface
step.action: Write interface (`InventoryChecker`, `PaymentGateway`) with methods named in domain language. Parameters and return types are domain types. No SDK imports.

step.name: implement-adapter
step.action: Implement the interface in `infrastructure/<system>_adapter.py`. This is the ONLY file importing the vendor SDK. Wrap SDK calls in try/except and translate to domain errors.

step.name: fail-safe-policy
step.action: Decide: assume-available with retry, deny-by-default, or cached-last-known. Document the default behaviour and rationale in a docstring on the adapter class. Add a runbook entry.

step.name: contract-and-domain-tests
step.action: Write a contract test using a mock HTTP server replaying canonical vendor responses. Write domain-side tests mocking the interface (no real SDK calls). Wire the adapter into DI.
step.decision-gate: If contract test passes against an outdated mock, re-record from the live API before merging.
